# Bluesky vs Threads for Founders in 2026

## Summary

Founder-focused comparison: Threads wins for discovery and audience growth (especially with existing Instagram following), while Bluesky wins for authentic engagement and early-mover advantage in tech and startup circles. Key difference: Threads is algorithm-driven discovery (can reach non-followers), Bluesky is chronological-default with custom feeds (community-curated discovery). Both crossed significant milestones in 2026: Bluesky surpassed 40M active users; Threads crossed 200M MAU.

## Key quotations / statistics

- "Bluesky surpassed 40 million active users and Threads crossed 200 million monthly actives" (2026)
- Bluesky avg engagement rate: 3-6% | Threads avg engagement rate: 1-3% (Q1 2026)
- Bluesky posting frequency needed: 3-5x/week | Threads: 5-7x/week minimum for meaningful reach
- "Bluesky's default feeds are largely chronological. A well-timed post gets seen without needing to constantly 'feed the algorithm.'"
- "Early-mover advantage: With ~40 million users, Bluesky is smaller — and that means less noise. Founders building a presence now are acquiring followers before the platform scales."
- "No ads (yet): Zero paid promotion means the playing field is level. Organic, value-first content wins outright."
- "Algorithm-driven discovery: Threads actively pushes content to new audiences. A single post with good engagement can reach tens of thousands of non-followers."
- "Algorithmic unpredictability: Meta's algorithm is a black box. What drives reach one week can tank the next."
- "Choose Bluesky if: Your target customers are developers, founders, or tech-adjacent professionals. You want to build authentic relationships, not just broadcast content."
- "Choose Threads if: You already have an Instagram following. Your customers are general consumers or lifestyle-adjacent buyers."

## Platform comparison table (founder view)

| Factor | Bluesky | Threads |
|---|---|---|
| Monthly Active Users | ~40M | ~200M |
| Feed Type | Chronological (default) | Algorithmic |
| Audience Profile | Tech, dev, early adopters | Consumer, lifestyle, general |
| Organic Reach | High (less competition) | Variable (algorithm-dependent) |
| Best For | B2B, SaaS, dev tools | B2C, consumer brands |
| Posting frequency needed | 3-5x/week | 5-7x/week |

## Annotations for stinger-forge

- **For `guides/02-platform-selection.md`:** The Bluesky vs Threads decision for solo developers almost always points to Bluesky first (tech audience, lower noise, authentic community). The stinger should surface this recommendation explicitly rather than leaving it as a "it depends."
- **For `guides/06-content-calendar.md`:** The posting frequency difference is material for calendar planning. Bluesky at 3-5x/week is sustainable for solo founders; Threads at 5-7x/week minimum is a high commitment bar.
- **For `guides/07-growth-benchmarks.md`:** Bluesky's 3-6% engagement rate vs Threads' 1-3% is important context for setting founder expectations — smaller platform but more quality engagement per post.
- **For `guides/00-principles.md`:** Bluesky's "no ads, chronological feed, level playing field" is directly aligned with the anti-gaming-algorithms philosophy of this Bee.
